justification: The requirement refers to clauses 5.3, 5.4, 5.5, 7.5, 7.6, and 9.4 of the standard, which are not provided for evaluation. Therefore, compliance cannot be assessed.
title: General requirements
summary: Compliance with the requirements of clauses 5.3, 5.4, 5.5, 7.5, 7.6 and 9.4 MUST be ensured.
applicable_if: IF controllers do not switch off the supply to a motor when it is at rest

justification: The requirement refers to Clause 11 of the standard, which is not provided for evaluation. Therefore, compliance cannot be assessed.
title: General requirements
summary: Motor control equipment MUST be located and mounted in accordance with Clause 11.

justification: The motor datasheet (Motor TDS.pdf, p. 7) specifies a standard protection rating of IP55. The starter enclosure datasheet (Boitier_contacteur_LE1D09P7A04.pdf, p. 1) specifies IP65. The magnetic sensor (Capteur magnetique - FSMA2.pdf, p. 1) is rated IP67. These degrees of protection are suitable for the food industry environment as described in the user manual (User manual FA.pdf, p. 10).
title: Motor enclosures
summary: The degree of protection for motor enclosures MUST be dependent on the application and the physical environment.

justification: The exploded view (F45899-4589.pdf, p. 1) shows the motor (part 1055) is installed within the machine's frame, protected by various covers (parts 5, 6, 13), which provides adequate protection from mechanical damage. This is supported by the sales leaflet (Sales leaflet-FA.pdf, p. 2) which mentions "CarÃ©nage total" (total casing).
title: Motor enclosures
summary: All motors MUST be adequately protected from mechanical damage.

justification: The motor technical datasheet (Motor TDS.pdf, p. 7) indicates the motor is a "moteur CEI," which implies adherence to IEC standards. However, there is no explicit declaration of conformity to the IEC 60072 series for motor dimensions.
title: Motor dimensions
summary: The dimensions of motors MUST conform to those given in the IEC 60072 series.

justification: The exploded view (F45899-4589.pdf, p. 1) shows the motor (1055), pulleys (49, 48, 47, 46, 44), and belt (1017) are all located inside the frame, protected by covers (5, 6, 13). The sales leaflet (Sales leaflet-FA.pdf, p. 2) confirms "CarÃ©nage total" (total casing).
title: Motor mounting and compartments
summary: Each motor and its associated couplings, belts, pulleys, or chains MUST be mounted so that they are adequately protected.

justification: The user manual (User manual FA.pdf, pp. 18-20) provides instructions for maintenance, including accessing internal components by opening the front cover or removing the side covers. This demonstrates that the motor and its associated drive components are accessible for inspection and maintenance.
title: Motor mounting and compartments
summary: Each motor and its associated couplings, belts, pulleys, or chains MUST be mounted so that they are easily accessible for inspection, maintenance, adjustment and alignment, lubrication, and replacement.

justification: The motor is listed as a replaceable component in the Bill of Materials (BOM FA2003T 2022.pdf, item 1055). The user manual (User manual FA.pdf, p. 19) specifies the removal of side covers for maintenance, which allows access to the motor, its mounting means, and its terminal box.
title: Motor mounting and compartments
summary: The motor mounting arrangement MUST be such that all motor mounting means can be removed and all terminal boxes are accessible.

justification: The motor is fan-cooled and has a class F insulation (Motor TDS.pdf, p. 7). However, the motor is located inside a total enclosure ("CarÃ©nage total" - Sales leaflet-FA.pdf, p. 2). The technical file does not provide any information, drawings, or photos to demonstrate that the motor compartment is adequately ventilated to ensure proper cooling and that the temperature rise remains within the limits of the insulation class.
title: Motor mounting and compartments
summary: Motors MUST be mounted so that proper cooling is ensured and the temperature rise remains within the limits of the insulation class.

justification: The motor is fan-cooled and located inside an enclosure. Ventilation of the motor compartment is therefore required. The technical file does not contain any drawings, photos, or descriptions of vents that would ventilate the motor compartment directly to the exterior of the machine.
title: Motor mounting and compartments
summary: Motor compartments MUST be ventilated directly to the exterior of the machine.

justification: Based on the provided documentation, there is no evidence that the motor compartment is ventilated. Therefore, this requirement concerning the design of vents is not applicable.
title: Motor mounting and compartments
summary: The vents of motor compartments MUST be such that ingress of swarf, dust, or water spray is at an acceptable level.

justification: The technical file lacks drawings or descriptions of the internal compartments of the machine. It is not possible to verify if there are openings between the motor compartment and other compartments, or to assess the requirements of those other compartments.
title: Motor mounting and compartments
summary: There MUST be no opening between the motor compartment and any other compartment that does not meet the motor compartment requirements.

justification: The technical file does not provide drawings or information about the routing of conduits or pipes between different compartments. Therefore, it is not possible to verify if clearances are sealed as required.
title: Motor mounting and compartments
summary: Any clearance around a conduit or pipe MUST be sealed.
applicable_if: IF a conduit or pipe is run into the motor compartment from another compartment not meeting the motor compartment requirements

justification: The motor and associated equipment characteristics are appropriate for the intended use in the food industry. The motor power is 0.55 kW (User manual FA.pdf, p. 26), the starter is rated for 4 kW (Boitier_contacteur_LE1D09P7A04.pdf, p. 1), and the thermal overload relay is adjustable from 1-1.6A (relais_thermique_SCH034676_FICHE_1.pdf, p. 1), which correctly covers the motor's nominal current of 1.41A. The IP ratings (IP55 for motor, IP65 for starter) are suitable for the environment.
title: Criteria for motor selection
summary: The characteristics of motors and associated equipment MUST be selected in accordance with the anticipated service and physical environmental conditions.

justification: The documentation demonstrates that motor selection criteria were considered. The motor is a standard asynchronous type for fixed-speed, S1 duty operation (Motor TDS.pdf, p. 155). The gearmotor selection guide (Motor TDS.pdf, p. 19) provides a detailed methodology for choosing the unit based on a service factor that accounts for operating time, starting frequency, and load inertia.
title: Criteria for motor selection
summary: The following points MUST be considered for motor selection: type of motor; type of duty cycle (see IEC 60034-1); fixed speed or variable speed operation, (and the consequent variable influence of the ventilation); mechanical vibration; type of motor control; temperature rise and other effects of the frequency spectrum of the voltage and/or current feeding the motor (particularly when it is supplied from a converter); method of starting and the possible influence of the inrush current on the operation of other users of the same power supply, taking also into account possible special considerations stipulated by the supply authority; variation of counter-torque load with time and speed; influence of loads with large inertia; influence of constant torque or constant power operation; possible need of inductive reactors between motor and converter.

justification: The technical documentation (BOM FA2003T 2022.pdf, Motor TDS.pdf, User manual FA.pdf) does not indicate the presence of a mechanical brake on the motor or the machine. Therefore, this requirement is not applicable.
title: Protective devices for mechanical brakes
summary: Operation of the overload and overcurrent protective devices for mechanical brake actuators MUST initiate the simultaneous de-energization (release) of the associated machine actuators.

title: Marking, warning signs and reference designations

justification: The technical file mentions durable materials like high-resistance epoxy coating (User manual FA.pdf, p. 11) and screen-printed plates (BOM FA2003T 2022.pdf, p. 1, item 74). However, there is no specific evidence, such as material specifications for labels or durability test reports (e.g., abrasion, fluid resistance), to confirm that all markings, signs, and plates can withstand the physical environment involved for the foreseen lifetime.
title: General
summary: Warning signs, nameplates, markings, labels and identification plates MUST be of sufficient durability to withstand the physical environment involved.

justification: The technical file shows examples of markings (e.g., User manual FA.pdf, p. 14; Motor TDS.pdf, p. 154). However, it lacks specific evidence like material data for labels or durability test reports (e.g., abrasion resistance) to prove that these markings will remain legible for the foreseen lifetime of the machine.
title: General
summary: The markings MUST be sufficiently durable to remain legible for the foreseen lifetime of the machine.

justification: The machine contains an electrical enclosure with hazardous voltage. Product photos (Sales leaflet-FA.pdf, p. 1; Technical data sheet FA.pdf, p. 1) show a triangular warning sign on the enclosure. However, the symbol used is the general warning symbol (ISO 7010-W001, exclamation mark), not the required graphical symbol for electric shock hazard (ISO 7010-W012, lightning bolt).
title: Electric shock hazard
summary: Enclosures that do not otherwise clearly show that they contain electrical equipment that can give rise to a risk of electric shock MUST be marked with the graphical symbol ISO 7010-W012.
applicable_if: IF enclosures do not otherwise clearly show that they contain electrical equipment that can give rise to a risk of electric shock AND the enclosure is NOT equipped with a supply disconnecting device AND it is NOT an operator-machine interface or control station AND it is NOT a single device with its own enclosure

justification: The product photos (Sales leaflet-FA.pdf, p. 1; Technical data sheet FA.pdf, p. 1) show that a warning sign is placed on the front of the electrical enclosure and is plainly visible.
title: Electric shock hazard
summary: The warning sign MUST be plainly visible on the enclosure door or cover.
applicable_if: IF a warning sign for electric shock hazard is required

justification: The technical file, including the user manual's safety section (User manual FA.pdf, p. 15), does not identify any risk of hazardous surface temperatures that would necessitate a warning sign. No risk assessment is provided to indicate otherwise.
title: Hot surfaces hazard
summary: The graphical symbol ISO 7010-W017 MUST be used.
applicable_if: IF the risk assessment shows the need to warn against the possibility of hazardous surface temperatures of the electrical equipment

justification: The user manual shows a photo of the adjustment levers clearly marked with their functions "ALLONGEMENT" and "LAMINAGE" (User manual FA.pdf, p. 14). Product photos (e.g., Technical data sheet FA.pdf, p. 1) show the start and stop buttons are identified by color (green/red), which is a recognized form of functional identification.
title: Functional identification
summary: Control devices and visual indicators MUST be clearly and durably marked with regard to their functions either on or adjacent to the item.

justification: The supplier's trademark, "REDACTED", is clearly marked on the front of the machine enclosure and is plainly visible in multiple product photos (e.g., Sales leaflet-FA.pdf, p. 1; Technical data sheet FA.pdf, p. 1).
title: Marking of enclosures of electrical equipment
summary: The name or trade mark of the supplier MUST be legibly and durably marked in a way that is plainly visible after the equipment is installed.
applicable_if: FOR enclosures that receive incoming power supplies

justification: The technical file does not contain any photo or inspection report showing the machine's main nameplate. The product photos in the leaflets and manuals do not show the type designation or model (e.g., FA2003T) marked on the equipment.
title: Marking of enclosures of electrical equipment
summary: The type designation or model MUST be legibly and durably marked in a way that is plainly visible after the equipment is installed.
applicable_if: IF applicable, FOR enclosures that receive incoming power supplies

justification: The technical file does not contain a photo or inspection report of the machine's main nameplate to verify that a serial number is marked on the enclosure.
title: Marking of enclosures of electrical equipment
summary: The serial number MUST be legibly and durably marked in a way that is plainly visible after the equipment is installed.
applicable_if: IF applicable, FOR enclosures that receive incoming power supplies

justification: The technical file does not contain a photo or inspection report of the machine's main nameplate to verify that the main document number (e.g., user manual number) is marked on the enclosure.
title: Marking of enclosures of electrical equipment
summary: The main document number MUST be legibly and durably marked in a way that is plainly visible after the equipment is installed.
applicable_if: IF applicable, FOR enclosures that receive incoming power supplies

justification: While the electrical ratings are specified in several documents (e.g., "380V/3+N/50Hz", "0.55 kW" in Sales leaflet-FA.pdf, p. 2; "400 V triphasÃ©" in User manual FA.pdf, p. 11), there is no evidence in the form of a photo or inspection report of the machine's nameplate to verify that these ratings are physically marked on the enclosure.
title: Marking of enclosures of electrical equipment
summary: The rated voltage, number of phases and frequency (if AC), and full-load current for each incoming supply MUST be legibly and durably marked in a way that is plainly visible after the equipment is installed.
applicable_if: FOR enclosures that receive incoming power supplies

justification: The technical documentation uses reference designations for components in the exploded view (F45899-4589.pdf) and the electrical schematic (User manual FA.pdf, p. 26). However, the technical file lacks evidence, such as photos of internal wiring and components or an inspection report, to verify that the physical parts are marked with these same reference designations.
title: Reference designations
summary: All enclosures, assemblies, control devices, and components MUST be plainly identified with the same reference designation as shown in the technical documentation.
